items.title: USAID — Identity in a Digital Age: Infrastructure for Inclusive Development
items.summary: Guidance on addressing systemic problems in digital ID systems and how emerging technologies will shape the future of digital identity for inclusive development.

items.title: USAID — Digital Identity Program
items.summary: USAID's digital identity program page covering risk assessments, inclusive development frameworks, and the role of digital ID in unlocking services like voting, financial access, and land titling.

items.title: NIST SP 800-63-3 — Digital Identity Guidelines
items.summary: Four-volume suite covering identity proofing, authentication, lifecycle management, and federation. The foundational federal standard for digital identity assurance levels.

items.title: DHS S&T — Next-Generation Identity: Mobile Driver's License Fact Sheet
items.summary: Fact sheet on DHS efforts to evaluate mobile driver's licenses (mDLs) as a next-generation identity credential for federal acceptance.

items.title: DHS S&T — Identity Management (IDM) Program
items.summary: Overview of DHS S&T's identity management research portfolio, including standards development, testing, and interoperability for digital credentials.

items.title: TSA — Digital Identity and Facial Comparison Technology
items.summary: TSA's program for accepting mobile driver's licenses and digital IDs at airport checkpoints. Covers supported wallets, participating states, privacy protections, and REAL ID compliance.

items.title: W3C — Decentralized Identifiers (DIDs) v1.0
items.summary: The core specification for DIDs — globally unique identifiers that enable verifiable, decentralized digital identity.

items.title: W3C — Verifiable Credentials Data Model v2.0
items.summary: The standard for expressing tamper-evident credentials on the web — licenses, certifications, clearances — that can be cryptographically verified.

items.title: NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5 — Security and Privacy Controls
items.summary: The catalog of security and privacy controls for federal information systems. Blockchain deployments must map to applicable control families (AU, AC, SC, IA).

items.title: DHS S&T — Blockchain Portfolio (SVIP)
items.summary: Overview of DHS Silicon Valley Innovation Program's blockchain investments, including supply chain traceability, verifiable credentials, and interoperability testing.

items.title: JFMIP — Federal Grant Payment Blockchain Initiative
items.summary: Final report on the Treasury/NSF/GAO blockchain prototype for near real-time grant payment visibility across agency boundaries.

items.title: JFMIP-24-01 — Harnessing Blockchain in the Federal Government
items.meta: PDF · JFMIP (Treasury, GAO, OMB, OPM) · December 2023
items.summary: Key considerations for financial management and information systems. Covers the grants financial management blockchain prototype, IT and cybersecurity factors, ATO considerations, governance models, human capital impact, and audit implications.

items.title: EU Expert Group on Blockchain Ethics
items.summary: Independent group of ten specialists in blockchain, philosophy, computer ethics, and law. Developed official EU guidelines addressing fairness, privacy, security, economic accountability, and societal responsibility.

items.title: Ethical Guidelines for Blockchain Systems
items.meta: Publication · PDF · EU Expert Group on Blockchain Ethics · 2024
items.summary: The full guidelines document covering fairness, privacy, security, economic accountability, and societal responsibility for blockchain deployments within the EU framework.
